content:
# Numbers Chapter 1 -- The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)

**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 145-147; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 110-111 | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_001_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_001_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_001_page_003.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/NUM_001_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_001_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

## YHWH Commands the Census (vv. 1-4)

**1** And YHWH spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the tent of meeting, on the first day of the second month, in the second year of their going out from the land of Egypt, saying:

**2** "Lift up the head of all the congregation of the sons of Israel, by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names -- every male, by their heads.

**3** From twenty years old and upward, all who go out to the host in Israel -- you shall muster them by their hosts, you and Aaron.

**4** And with you there shall be one man from each tribe, each one the head of the house of his fathers.

---

## The Tribal Leaders Named (vv. 5-16)

**5** And these are the names of the men who shall stand with you: for Reuben -- Elizur son of Shedeur;

**6** for Simeon -- Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai;

**7** for Judah -- Nahshon son of Amminadab;

**8** for Issachar -- Nethanel son of Zuar;

**9** for Zebulun -- Eliab son of Helon;

**10** for the sons of Joseph: for Ephraim -- Elishama son of Ammihud; for Manasseh -- Gamaliel son of Pedahzur;

**11** for Benjamin -- Abidan son of Gideoni;

**12** for Dan -- Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai;

**13** for Asher -- Pagiel son of Ochran;

**14** for Gad -- Eliasaph son of Deuel;

**15** for Naphtali -- Ahira son of Enan.

**16** These were the called ones of the congregation, the leaders of the tribes of their fathers, the heads of the thousands of Israel.

---

## Moses and Aaron Muster the Assembly (vv. 17-19)

**17** And Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name,

**18** and they assembled all the congregation on the first day of the second month. And they registered their lineage by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, by their heads,

**19** as YHWH had commanded Moses. And he mustered them in the wilderness of Sinai.

---

## The Tribal Census (vv. 20-43)

**20** And the sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel -- their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, by their heads, every male from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**21** their mustered ones of the tribe of Reuben: forty-six thousand and five hundred.

**22** Of the sons of Simeon -- their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, his mustered ones by the number of names, by their heads, every male from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**23** their mustered ones of the tribe of Simeon: fifty-nine thousand and three hundred.

**24** Of the sons of Gad -- their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**25** their mustered ones of the tribe of Gad: forty-five thousand and six hundred and fifty.

**26** Of the sons of Judah -- their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**27** their mustered ones of the tribe of Judah: seventy-four thousand and six hundred.

**28** Of the sons of Issachar -- their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**29** their mustered ones of the tribe of Issachar: fifty-four thousand and four hundred.

**30** Of the sons of Zebulun -- their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**31** their mustered ones of the tribe of Zebulun: fifty-seven thousand and four hundred.

**32** Of the sons of Joseph: of the sons of Ephraim -- their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**33** their mustered ones of the tribe of Ephraim: forty thousand and five hundred.

**34** Of the sons of Manasseh -- their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**35** their mustered ones of the tribe of Manasseh: thirty-two thousand and two hundred.

**36** Of the sons of Benjamin -- their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**37** their mustered ones of the tribe of Benjamin: thirty-five thousand and four hundred.

**38** Of the sons of Dan -- their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**39** their mustered ones of the tribe of Dan: sixty-two thousand and seven hundred.

**40** Of the sons of Asher -- their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**41** their mustered ones of the tribe of Asher: forty-one thousand and five hundred.

**42** Of the sons of Naphtali -- their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**43** their mustered ones of the tribe of Naphtali: fifty-three thousand and four hundred.

---

## The Census Total (vv. 44-46)

**44** These are the ones who were mustered, whom Moses and Aaron mustered, and the leaders of Israel -- twelve men, each one for the house of his fathers.

**45** And all the mustered ones of the sons of Israel, by the houses of their fathers, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host in Israel --

**46** and all the mustered ones were six hundred and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.

---

## The Levites Exempted (vv. 47-51)

**47** But the Levites, by the tribe of their fathers, were not mustered among them.

**48** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:

**49** "Only the tribe of Levi you shall not muster, and their head you shall not lift up among the sons of Israel.

**50** But you shall appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the testimony, and over all its vessels, and over all that belongs to it. They shall carry the tabernacle and all its vessels, and they shall minister to it, and they shall encamp around the tabernacle.

**51** And when the tabernacle sets out, the Levites shall take it down; and when the tabernacle encamps, the Levites shall set it up. And the stranger who comes near shall be put to death.

---

## The Camp Arrangement and Conclusion (vv. 52-54)

**52** And the sons of Israel shall encamp, each man by his camp and each man by his standard, according to their hosts.

**53** But the Levites shall encamp around the tabernacle of the testimony, so that there shall be no wrath upon the congregation of the sons of Israel. And the Levites shall keep the charge of the tabernacle of the testimony.

**54** And the sons of Israel did according to all that YHWH had commanded Moses; so they did.

---

## SOPHIA's Consensus Notes

### Manuscript Confirmation
The Leningrad Codex pages 145-147 (West Semitic Research Project photographs by Bruce E. Zuckerman) present Numbers 1 in three-column format with Masorah parva in the margins. Page 145 opens the book with vaydabber YHWH el-Mosheh be-midbar Sinai. The tribal census formulae on pages 145-146 display the highly repetitive pattern with only tribe names and numbers varying. Page 147 carries through the Levitical exemption to the chapter's close. The Vaticanus LXX (Biblioteca Apostolica Vaticana, Vat.gr.1209, pp. 110-111) presents the Greek Arithmoi in three-column uncial. The chapter heading CAP. X is visible at the margin of page 110. The Greek text follows the MT tribal order and all twelve census figures match exactly. The LXX total at v. 46 is identical: 603,550.

### Key Translation Decisions

1. **"Lift up the head" (v. 2):** EMET renders the census idiom literally as "lift up the head"; LOGOS formalizes as "take up the sum"; PNEUMA dynamizes as "take a census." SOPHIA adopts EMET's literal rendering "lift up the head" because the Hebrew idiom se'u et-rosh is theologically resonant -- it appears in Genesis 40:13, 19-20 with the dual sense of exaltation and execution. Preserving the bodily metaphor retains this intertextual depth. The LXX's labete archen ("take a sum") confirms the census meaning but loses the Hebrew metaphor.

2. **"Muster" / "mustered ones" (throughout):** The root paqad governs the entire chapter. EMET renders it "muster" (verbal) and "mustered-ones" (nominal); LOGOS uses "number" and "numbered ones"; PNEUMA uses "count" and "number counted." SOPHIA adopts "muster" / "mustered ones" because paqad carries a semantic range beyond mere counting -- it means to attend to, visit, appoint, and charge with responsibility. "Muster" in English preserves the military-covenantal sense: YHWH musters His host, not merely counts a population. The LXX's episkeptomai ("to visit/inspect") confirms this richer meaning.

3. **"Host" (vv. 3, 20-43, 45, 52):** All three translators agree on "host" for tsava' in the census formula. PNEUMA alone departs by rendering "fit for military service" for kol yotse' tsava'. SOPHIA retains "all who went out to the host" for the full phrase, following the EMET/LOGOS consensus, because tsava' encompasses organized assembly under divine command beyond strict military service.

4. **"Registered their lineage" (v. 18):** EMET renders vayyityaledu as "they registered their lineage"; LOGOS as "they registered themselves"; PNEUMA as "every man... declared his ancestry." SOPHIA adopts "they registered their lineage" from EMET because the Hithpael of yalad ("to beget") specifically denotes declaration of birth-descent, not generic self-registration. The lineage element is inherent in the verbal root and should be made explicit.

5. **"Called ones" (v. 16):** EMET and LOGOS render qeru'ei ha-edah as "the called ones of the congregation"; PNEUMA renders "the ones chosen from the community." SOPHIA follows the EMET/LOGOS literal rendering because qeru'ei is a passive participle of qara' ("to call"), not bakhar ("to choose"). These men are summoned, not elected.

6. **"Head" vs. "sum" (v. 49):** EMET renders ve-et-rosham lo' tissa' as "their head you shall not lift up"; LOGOS renders "their sum you shall not take up"; PNEUMA renders "you must not include them in the census." SOPHIA renders "their head you shall not lift up" to maintain consistency with v. 2 (se'u et-rosh) -- the same census idiom, now applied in the negative. This connects the Levitical exemption directly to the census command and preserves the Hebrew wordplay.

7. **"Stranger" (v. 51):** EMET and LOGOS render ha-zar as "the stranger"; PNEUMA renders "any outsider." SOPHIA follows "the stranger" -- the formal equivalent of zar -- because "outsider" imports a modern sociological register absent from the Hebrew. The term zar denotes one who is "other" in the cultic sense (non-Levite), and "stranger" in its older English sense captures this precisely.

8. **"Standard" (v. 52):** EMET and LOGOS render diglo as "his standard"; PNEUMA renders "his own banner." SOPHIA follows "his standard" because degel in Numbers denotes the military-organizational ensign of each tribal division. "Standard" in English carries this military denotation. "Banner" is not incorrect but skews toward decorative rather than military function.

9. **Census formula consistency:** The formulaic repetition of vv. 20-43 is theologically purposeful, treating each tribe with identical dignity in YHWH's muster. SOPHIA preserves the full formula for every tribe (following LOGOS) rather than condensing it (as EMET does after Reuben) or paraphrasing it (as PNEUMA does). The repetition is the text's own rhetoric and must not be editorially reduced.

### Consistent Term Renderings (Numbers Series)

| Hebrew | SOPHIA Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| benei Yisra'el | sons of Israel | Binding convention; not "children" or "Israelites" |
| YHWH | YHWH | Transliterated; binding convention |
| se'u et-rosh | lift up the head | Census idiom preserved literally |
| paqad / pequdim | muster / mustered ones | Covenantal-military range of paqad |
| tsava' | host | Military assembly; not "army" or "service" |
| matteh | tribe | Administrative unit |
| mishpachah | clan | Kinship unit between tribe and father's house |
| beit av / beit avot | house of his/their fathers | Patrilineal household |
| gulgolet | head (by their heads) | Lit. "skull"; individual poll-count |
| toledot | generations | Genealogical registry |
| nasi' / nesi'im | leader / leaders | Tribal chieftain; from nasa' ("to lift up") |
| mishkan ha-edut | tabernacle of the testimony | The covenant tablets define the structure |
| zar | stranger | Non-Levite in sacral context |
| qetsef | wrath | Divine wrath; not softened to "anger" |
| degel | standard | Tribal military ensign |
| mishmeret | charge | Guard-duty; from shamar ("to keep") |

--- SOPHIA
